authored.para: This instruction loads a vector register from a memory address generated by a 64-bit scalar base, plus an immediate offset in the range -256 to 255 that is multiplied by the current vector register size in bytes. This instruction is unpredicated.

The load is performed as contiguous byte accesses, with no endian conversion and no guarantee of single-copy atomicity larger than a byte. However, if alignment is checked, then the base register must be aligned to 16 bytes.

uses_dit.condition: FEAT_SVE2 is implemented or FEAT_SME is implemented

operationalnote_content.para: This instruction is a data-independent-time instruction as described in About PSTATE.DIT.

a.hover: Is the name of the scalable vector register to be transferred, encoded in the "Zt" field.
Is the 64-bit name of the general-purpose base register or stack pointer, encoded in the "Rn" field.
Is the optional signed immediate vector offset, in the range -256 to 255, defaulting to 0, encoded in the "imm9h:imm9l" fields.

ps.name: A64.sve.sve_mem32.sve_mem_32b_fill.ldr_z_bi_

intro.para: Is the name of the scalable vector register to be transferred, encoded in the "Zt" field.
Is the 64-bit name of the general-purpose base register or stack pointer, encoded in the "Rn" field.
Is the optional signed immediate vector offset, in the range -256 to 255, defaulting to 0, encoded in the "imm9h:imm9l" fields.

ps.name: A64.sve.sve_mem32.sve_mem_32b_fill.ldr_z_bi_
